verses.text: Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah, even Ephraim and Manasseh, to keep the Passover, held in the second month because it could not be kept in the first. He gathers the scattered to worship. God's grace reaches across old divisions to call every tribe back to His table.

Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah, even Ephraim and Manasseh, to keep the Passover, held in the second month because it could not be kept in the first. He gathers the scattered to worship. God's grace reaches across old divisions to call every tribe back to His table.

Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah, even Ephraim and Manasseh, to keep the Passover, held in the second month because it could not be kept in the first. He gathers the scattered to worship. God's grace reaches across old divisions to call every tribe back to His table.

Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah, even Ephraim and Manasseh, to keep the Passover, held in the second month because it could not be kept in the first. He gathers the scattered to worship. God's grace reaches across old divisions to call every tribe back to His table.

Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah, even Ephraim and Manasseh, to keep the Passover, held in the second month because it could not be kept in the first. He gathers the scattered to worship. God's grace reaches across old divisions to call every tribe back to His table.

The couriers carry his letters: 'turn again unto the LORD... and he will return to the remnant,' for 'the LORD your God is gracious and merciful.' The summons is all mercy. God promises that the moment His people turn to Him, He turns to them.

The couriers carry his letters: 'turn again unto the LORD... and he will return to the remnant,' for 'the LORD your God is gracious and merciful.' The summons is all mercy. God promises that the moment His people turn to Him, He turns to them.

The couriers carry his letters: 'turn again unto the LORD... and he will return to the remnant,' for 'the LORD your God is gracious and merciful.' The summons is all mercy. God promises that the moment His people turn to Him, He turns to them.

The couriers carry his letters: 'turn again unto the LORD... and he will return to the remnant,' for 'the LORD your God is gracious and merciful.' The summons is all mercy. God promises that the moment His people turn to Him, He turns to them.

Some of Ephraim and Manasseh mock the couriers, but others humble themselves and come, while in Judah 'the hand of God was to give them one heart' to obey. Grace divides the scornful from the humble. Where God works, hearts are knit together in glad obedience.

Some of Ephraim and Manasseh mock the couriers, but others humble themselves and come, while in Judah 'the hand of God was to give them one heart' to obey. Grace divides the scornful from the humble. Where God works, hearts are knit together in glad obedience.

Some of Ephraim and Manasseh mock the couriers, but others humble themselves and come, while in Judah 'the hand of God was to give them one heart' to obey. Grace divides the scornful from the humble. Where God works, hearts are knit together in glad obedience.

A great congregation gathers, removes the pagan altars from Jerusalem, and the priests, ashamed, sanctify themselves. The people's zeal shames the slow into holiness. A wave of devotion sweeps away idols and stirs even the careless to prepare their hearts.

A great congregation gathers, removes the pagan altars from Jerusalem, and the priests, ashamed, sanctify themselves. The people's zeal shames the slow into holiness. A wave of devotion sweeps away idols and stirs even the careless to prepare their hearts.

A great congregation gathers, removes the pagan altars from Jerusalem, and the priests, ashamed, sanctify themselves. The people's zeal shames the slow into holiness. A wave of devotion sweeps away idols and stirs even the careless to prepare their hearts.

Because many in the crowd are not ceremonially clean, the Levites kill the passover lambs on their behalf. Love makes a way for the unready. God's ministers stoop to help the willing-hearted who fall short of the form, that none be shut out.

Because many in the crowd are not ceremonially clean, the Levites kill the passover lambs on their behalf. Love makes a way for the unready. God's ministers stoop to help the willing-hearted who fall short of the form, that none be shut out.

Many from Ephraim eat the Passover unprepared, and Hezekiah prays, 'the good LORD pardon every one that prepareth his heart to seek God,' and the LORD heals the people. God looks on the prepared heart above the perfect rite, pardoning the sincere who seek Him.

Many from Ephraim eat the Passover unprepared, and Hezekiah prays, 'the good LORD pardon every one that prepareth his heart to seek God,' and the LORD heals the people. God looks on the prepared heart above the perfect rite, pardoning the sincere who seek Him.

Many from Ephraim eat the Passover unprepared, and Hezekiah prays, 'the good LORD pardon every one that prepareth his heart to seek God,' and the LORD heals the people. God looks on the prepared heart above the perfect rite, pardoning the sincere who seek Him.

They keep the feast seven days with great gladness, and the Levites teach 'the good knowledge of the LORD.' Worship and teaching go hand in hand. Joy in God's presence is deepened when His truth is opened to the people's understanding.

They keep the feast seven days with great gladness, and the Levites teach 'the good knowledge of the LORD.' Worship and teaching go hand in hand. Joy in God's presence is deepened when His truth is opened to the people's understanding.

The whole assembly resolves to keep another seven days, and the king and princes give thousands of animals for the feast. Gladness multiplies devotion. When God's people taste His goodness, they are not eager to leave His presence but to linger longer.

The whole assembly resolves to keep another seven days, and the king and princes give thousands of animals for the feast. Gladness multiplies devotion. When God's people taste His goodness, they are not eager to leave His presence but to linger longer.

All rejoice, including the strangers among them, with a joy unseen since Solomon's day, and the priests bless the people, their prayer reaching God's holy dwelling. Wholehearted worship overflows in joy and is heard in heaven.
